claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children. Let me start with the basics.

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to check the specific rules. Let me do that first.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have the rules, let me ask you some key questions about Alex to determine your eligibility.

**Is Alex currently under 16 years old?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful information.

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. That's very useful to know.

**Are you responsible for looking after Alex and contributing to his upkeep (such as food, clothes, accommodation, and pocket money)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after Alex and provide for all his needs - food, clothes, everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just to confirm a few more important details:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else made a claim for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One more question to complete my assessment:

**Are you a British citizen, do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or do you have the right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided and the Child Benefit eligibility rules:

- You are responsible for Alex and live with him in the UK
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You provide for all his needs
- You are a British citizen
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

I'm pleased to inform you that you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! You are **eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

You meet all the necessary criteria:
- You are responsible for Alex and live with him
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK
- You are a British citizen
- No one else is claiming for him

You can now proceed with making a claim through HMRC. If you have any other questions about the claim process, you may want to contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) You are responsible for Alex as you live with him and provide for all his needs including food, clothes, and accommodation; (2) Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age of 16; (3) You live in the UK; (4) You are a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK; (5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so only one person (you) will receive it.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for your son Alex. You meet all the core eligibility requirements: you are responsible for Alex, live with him in the UK, he is under 16, you are a British citizen, and no one else is claiming for him. You should be able to proceed with your application to HMRC."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) You are responsible for Alex as you live with him and provide for all his needs including food, clothes, and accommodation; (2) Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age of 16; (3) You live in the UK; (4) You are a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK; (5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so only one person (you) will receive it.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for your son Alex. You meet all the core eligibility requirements: you are responsible for Alex, live with him in the UK, he is under 16, you are a British citizen, and no one else is claiming for him. You should be able to proceed with your application to HMRC."
